The opposite of "cheap" is:

options: I travel my homework every night.
She travel a new bag yesterday.
He travel the piano every day.
We travel to Busan by train.

question: I usually ___ breakfast at 7 a.m.
"He finished his homework early." — What does "finished" mean?
"She visits her grandmother every weekend." — What does "visits" mean?
Which word is closest in meaning to "angry"?
Don't forget to bring your ___ to board the airplane.
A ___ works in a hospital and helps sick people.
She ___ than her brother. (She is taller.)
"The museum was crowded on Saturday." — What does "crowded" mean?
"Collecting baseball cards was more popular in the past." — What does "popular" mean?
She was ___ because she won the competition.
The book was so interesting that I ___ it in one day.
The experiment ___ by the students last week.
"She regrets not studying harder." — What does "regrets" mean?
My father is known for his generosity. — What does "generosity" mean?
The documentary illustrated the importance of environmental conservation. — What does "conservation" mean?
Despite the bad weather, they continued their outdoor activities. — What does "despite" mean?
"Taking care of a pet is hard work, but Jisu feels happy when the puppy runs to her." — What is the main message?
options: Caring for a pet can be rewarding.

question: "The scientist made a remarkable discovery." — What does "remarkable" mean?
If she ___ harder, she would have passed the test.
"Not only did he finish the project, but he also helped his teammates." — This sentence emphasizes:
"The author's use of metaphor enhanced the story." — What is a "metaphor"?
"He is known for his perseverance." — What does "perseverance" mean?
"While technology has made communication easier, it has also led to a decline in face-to-face interaction." — What is the author's main point?
options: Technology has both benefits and drawbacks.

question: "The government implemented new policies to address climate change." — What does "implemented" mean?
"Her argument was compelling, but lacked concrete evidence." — What does "compelling" mean?
"Scientists are investigating the link between air pollution and cognitive decline." — What does "cognitive" relate to?
"The treaty was ratified by all member states." — What does "ratified" mean?
"She was lauded for her contribution to scientific research." — What does "lauded" mean?
"The findings were inconclusive due to insufficient data." — What does "inconclusive" mean?
"His eloquent speech moved the audience to tears." — What does "eloquent" mean?
"The policy was criticized for being too ambiguous." — What does "ambiguous" mean?
"The researcher's hypothesis was subsequently validated." — What does "subsequently" mean?
"The novel's protagonist undergoes a profound transformation." — What does "protagonist" mean?
"The legislation was designed to mitigate the adverse effects of industrialization." — What does "mitigate" mean?
"The committee deliberated for hours before reaching a consensus." — What does "deliberated" mean?
"Her unprecedented achievement earned her international recognition." — What does "unprecedented" mean?
"The company's disruptive innovation fundamentally altered consumer behavior." — What does "disruptive" mean here?
"The philosopher's treatise elucidated the relationship between morality and law." — What does "elucidated" mean?
"The recession had far-reaching ramifications for the global market." — What does "ramifications" mean?
"The novel employs an epistolary narrative structure." — What does "epistolary" mean?
"The diplomat's judicious response prevented further escalation." — What does "judicious" mean?
"The excavation yielded a plethora of ancient artifacts." — What does "plethora" mean?
"Her inexorable rise through the corporate ranks inspired her colleagues." — What does "inexorable" mean?
"The satirical work subtly critiqued contemporary societal norms." — What does "satirical" mean?
"The scientist's seminal research laid the groundwork for future discoveries." — What does "seminal" mean?
"The paradoxical nature of his argument undermined its credibility." — What does "paradoxical" mean?
"The government's fiscal policies were deemed imprudent by analysts." — What does "imprudent" mean?
"The legislation engendered significant controversy among constitutional scholars." — What does "engendered" mean?
"His perspicacious analysis earned him widespread respect." — What does "perspicacious" mean?
"The manifesto articulated a cogent argument for systemic reform." — What does "cogent" mean?
"The novel's denouement was both unexpected and deeply satisfying." — What does "denouement" mean?
"The committee's obfuscatory tactics frustrated the investigators." — What does "obfuscatory" mean?

Read: "My name is Jenny. I am seven years old. I have one brother and one sister. We live in a small house."
How many siblings does Jenny have?

Read: "Tom has a cat named Coco. Coco is orange and white. She likes to sleep on the sofa."
What color is Coco?

Read: "I go to school by bus every morning. School starts at 8 o'clock. My favorite class is art."
How does this person go to school?

Read: "Last weekend, Mike and his family went to the beach. They played in the sand and swam in the sea. Mike found a big shell and was very happy."
What did Mike find at the beach?

Read: "Lisa is learning to cook. She can make rice, salad, and soup. Her mom helps her every weekend. Lisa wants to be a chef someday."
What does Lisa want to be in the future?

Read: "Korea has four seasons. Summers are hot and humid. Winters are cold and snowy. Spring and fall are mild."
Which season is hot and humid?

Read: "Every Saturday, Kevin volunteers at the animal shelter. He feeds the dogs and walks them. Kevin says volunteering is the best part of his week."
Why does Kevin enjoy Saturdays?

Read: "Scientists say that reading for just 20 minutes a day can improve your vocabulary and memory. It also helps reduce stress."
According to the text, what is one benefit of daily reading?

Read: "In 1969, astronauts first walked on the moon. Neil Armstrong was the first person to step on the moon's surface and called it 'one small step for man.'"
Who was the first person to walk on the moon?

Read: "Social media helps people stay connected, but research shows it can also lead to feelings of anxiety and loneliness when people constantly compare themselves to others."
What is the main concern about social media mentioned?

Read: "The Great Wall of China was built over many centuries to protect against invasions. Today it is one of the most visited tourist attractions in the world."
What was the ORIGINAL purpose of the Great Wall?

Read: "Online learning lets students study at their own pace from any location. However, it requires strong self-discipline and can feel isolating without face-to-face interaction."
What is one DISADVANTAGE of online learning?
options: It requires self-discipline and can feel isolating

question:
Read: "Urban farming—growing food within cities—is gaining popularity as a solution to food insecurity. Rooftop gardens and vertical farms are being developed worldwide."
What problem is urban farming trying to solve?

Read: "Despite warnings about sleep deprivation, many students sacrifice sleep to study. Research shows that a well-rested brain performs significantly better on memory and problem-solving tasks."
What conclusion can be drawn from this text?
options: Sleeping is more beneficial than studying all night
Sleep deprivation has no real effects

question:
Read: "Planned obsolescence—designing products to break down quickly—encourages excessive consumption and contributes to environmental waste, yet it remains a common corporate strategy."
What is the author's attitude toward planned obsolescence?

Read: "Technological advancements in AI offer unprecedented efficiencies, yet simultaneously raise profound ethical questions regarding privacy, employment displacement, and the concentration of power among a few corporations."
What central tension does this passage describe?

Put these words in the correct order:

She ___ to school every day.
(Hint: go → correct form for she)
Put these words in the correct order:

I ___ two cats at home.
(Hint: have or has?)

A fish ___ swim.
(Hint: ability modal)

Yesterday, I ___ to the park.
(Hint: go → past tense)
Put these words in the correct order:

They are ___ the piano right now.
(Hint: play → -ing form)

It is going to ___ tomorrow.
(Hint: weather verb)

I ___ already read this book. It's great!
(Hint: have or has?)
Put these words in the correct order:
expected: The cake was made by my mom

question:
She asked me ___ I liked the food.
(Hint: a word meaning "whether")
Put these words in the correct order:
expected: He is the tallest student in class

question:
If I ___ more time, I would travel the world.
(Hint: have → past form for unreal condition)

She has ___ to Paris before.
(Hint: go → past participle)
Not only did she pass the test, ___ she also got the highest score.
Put these words in the correct order:
expected: The student who won first place received a trophy

question:
The project ___ finished by the team yesterday.
(Hint: be → past passive)

She suggested that he ___ early.
(Hint: come → subjunctive mood, no -s)
Put these words in the correct order:
expected: Had they arrived earlier they would not have missed the bus

question:
The report, ___ was submitted late, was still accepted.
(Hint: relative pronoun for things)

By next year, I ___ lived here for ten years.
(Hint: future perfect = will + have)

Despite the bad weather, they ___ their outdoor activities.
(Hint: continue → past tense)
